factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, driven by increased sales of cloud-connected products and higher customer adoption.

evidence.text: Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment for the year ended December 31, 2023 increased $292.4 million, or 44.4%, as we continue to add users and associated devices to our network.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with future contracted revenue increasing significantly, indicating strong demand.

evidence.text: As of December 31, 2023, we had approximately $7.1 billion of future contracted revenue, an increase from $4.6 billion as of December 31, 2022.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, which saw a significant increase in gross margin percentage.

evidence.text: Gross margin as a percentage of total segment net sales increased to 61.5% for the year ended December 31, 2023 from 59.5% for the year ended December 31, 2022.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with a 31.4% increase in total revenue, driven by strong performance across both reportable segments.

evidence.text: Our revenues for the year ended December 31, 2023 were $1.6 billion, an increase of $373.5 million, or 31.4%, from the prior year.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in TASER devices and Axon Body cameras, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net sales for Axon Body cameras and accessories increased $25.7 million on higher volume driven by demand for our next generation product, Axon Body 4, which began shipping at the end of the second quarter of 2023.

factors.summary: The Software and Sensors segment is growing fastest, driven by increased adoption of cloud-based solutions and higher customer engagement.

evidence.text: Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment for the year ended December 31, 2023 increased $292.4 million, or 44.4%, as we continue to add users and associated devices to our network.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by higher headcount and payroll taxes, which are creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Salaries, benefits and bonus expense for the year ended December 31, 2023 increased $54.2 million as compared to the prior year. Of the total increase, $32.1 million is attributable to an increase in salaries and related primarily to increased headcount, and $9.0 million is attributable to payroll taxes related to the vesting of the final three tranches of our 2019 eXponential Stock Performance Plan.

factors.summary: Gross margins are stable, while operating margins are improving due to revenue growth outpacing expense increases.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $226.7 million to $955.4 million for the year ended December 31, 2023 compared to $728.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin decreased slightly to 61.1% for 2023 from 61.2% for 2022.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, driven by scale efficiencies and premium product mix.

evidence.text: Within the Software and Sensors segment, hardware gross margin was 45.6% for the year ended December 31, 2023, compared to 42.1% for the same period in 2022, while service margin decreased to 72.6% from 73.3%, respectively, during each of those same time periods. The increase in gross margin was driven by a premium product mix and scale efficiencies, partially offset by a higher mix of lower-margin professional services revenue.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed, with provisions made for excess and obsolete items, but overall levels are increasing to support future sales.

evidence.text: During the year ended December 31, 2023, we recorded provisions to reduce inventories to their lower of cost and net realizable value of approximately $5.4 million compared to $1.5 million during the year ended December 31, 2022.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing, with a 29.9% increase in R&D expenses, driven by higher headcount and product development activities.

evidence.text: R&D expenses for the year ended December 31, 2023 increased $69.9 million, or 29.9%, as compared to the prior year.

factors.summary: New products, such as TASER 10 and Axon Body 4, are gaining traction, contributing to revenue growth and customer demand.

evidence.text: The increase in TASER devices (professional) revenue was the result of increased unit sales and premium product mix. We continue to see strong adoption of our next generation product, TASER 10, which began shipping in the first quarter of 2023. Net sales of Axon Body cameras and accessories increased $25.7 million on higher volume driven by demand for our next generation product, Axon Body 4, which began shipping at the end of the second quarter of 2023.
